body:
### Problem

The statusline item `%=`, which should create the "separation point between alignment sections" doesn't work inside a fixed-width item group.

This is a regression when used in `rulerformat` and the ruler is shown in the statusline.
The ruler does not have this issue when shown in the cmdline.

### Steps to reproduce

Minimal `.vimrc` to reproduce that works in both Neovim and vim:
```vim
set ruler
set laststatus=2
set rulerformat=%20(%l,%c%V%=%P%m%)
```

The problem only appears
- in the statusline (`laststatus=2`), but not in the cmdline (`laststatus=1`)
- if `%=` is inside a fixed-width item group, e.g. `%20(...%)`

NB: the default rulerformat `%-14.(%l,%c%V%) %P` (or rather the fall-back in the default statusline) does not have this issue because the left-alignment is not created with `%=`, but I use it to ensure a fixed length of the whole ruler since `%m` has variable length.

Interestingly, the statusline itself does not seem to have fully supported `%=` even before 0.12, not even in vim. I.e. the following `.vimrc` shows the same error both in vim and Neovim (0.11 or 0.12):
```vim
set laststatus=2
set statusline=%20(%l,%c%V%=%P%m%)
```
It would of course be nice to support this in the statusline directly too, as advertised in `:help statusline`.

### Expected behavior

The left part (line, column, virtual column) should be left-aligned, while the right part (percentage through file and modified flag) should be right-aligned. Instead, there is no space in between.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

0.12.2 (cannot reproduce with 0.11.7)

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

behaves the same in statusline, behaves differently in rulerformat inside statusline, vim 9.2

### Operating system/version

NixOS 25.11

### Terminal name/version

alacritty or tty

### $TERM environment variable

alacritty or linux

### Installation

nix with channel https://nixos.org/channels/nixpkgs-unstable
title: statusline does not support %= inside of fixed-width item group in rulerformat
